cards.back: A service or resource that is non-excludable and non-rivalrous, meaning it cannot be denied to anyone and its consumption does not decrease its availability
A situation in which individuals benefit from a public good without contributing to its provision, often leading to underprovision of the good
A situation in which individual self-interest leads to the degradation or overuse of a shared resource, resulting in a suboptimal outcome for all
A situation in which individuals have an incentive to free ride on others, leading to a lack of cooperation and a suboptimal outcome for the group
A theorem in game theory that states that, in the absence of transaction costs, parties will negotiate to a mutually beneficial agreement, regardless of the initial allocation of a resource
